title: gymnogramma calomelanos var. tartarea
intro: Gymnogramma calomelanos var. tartarea, also known as Gymnogramma or Tartare Pteris, is a type of flowering plant belonging to the family Pteridaceae, commonly found in tropical regions. Traditionally, Gymnogramma calomelanos var. tartarea is used in herbal medicine for its medicinal properties, particularly its antifungal and antibacterial properties, which have been found to be effective in treating skin conditions and infections. The plant is believed to offer various health benefits, including the treatment of eczema, acne, and other skin disorders, as well as its potential anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties. Gymnogramma calomelanos var. tartarea is often prepared as a tea or tincture, and its leaves are also used in traditional medicine to make topical creams and ointments for skin care and wound healing.
